AIM & SCOPE: 2D Materials aims to publish high-quality, high-impact research in two-dimensional materials science and engineering, serving a multidisciplinary research community. The journal covers fundamental properties, synthesis and fabrication techniques, and advanced applications of 2D materials in electronics, mechanics, chemistry, energy, and biomedicine, with materials of interest including graphene and its derivatives, boron nitride, transition metal dichalcogenides, silicene, germanene, 2D topological insulators, complex oxides, composite materials, and other emerging layered structures, and publishes original research articles, Perspectives, and invited Topical Reviews of broad scientific importance.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: 2D Materials (2DM) accepts high-quality manuscripts that report original and impactful research in two-dimensional materials science and engineering, including full research articles presenting significant experimental, theoretical, or computational advances; letters describing urgent breakthrough results; Perspectives offering expert insights into emerging trends and future directions; and invited Topical Reviews on themes of current importance to the 2D materials community, with all submissions expected to demonstrate strong novelty, scientific rigor, broad multidisciplinary relevance, and substantial impact.
SUBJECT AREA: Materials Science - Electronic, Optical And Magnetic Materials; Surfaces, Coatings And Films; Physics And Astronomy - Condensed Matter Physics

AIM & SCOPE: 3 Biotech aims to publish high-quality research on the study and application of biotechnology across medicine and biomedical sciences, agriculture, and the environment, emphasizing the integration of diverse biotechnological techniques to address real-world challenges. The journal covers a broad range of topics including cancer and stem cell research, genetic engineering and cloning, bioremediation and biodegradation, bioinformatics and systems biology, biomarkers and biosensors, biodiversity and biodiscovery, biorobotics, biotoxins, and analytical biotechnology including human genome studies, and serves scientists and engineers in both academia and industry focused on the safe, efficient, and innovative application of biotechnology.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscript submission to the journal confirms that the work is original, has not been published or submitted elsewhere, and has been approved by all authors and the relevant institution. Authors must obtain permission for any previously published figures, tables, or text and provide proof during submission. Manuscripts are submitted online through the journals submission system, and authors must upload all required editable source files (such as .docx or LaTeX), as incomplete or non-editable files will not be considered for review.
SUBJECT AREA: Biochemistry, Genetics And Molecular Biology - Biotechnology; Immunology And Microbiology - Applied Microbiology And Biotechnology

AIM & SCOPE: 3D Printing and Additive Manufacturing aims to publish high-quality, peer-reviewed research covering both academic and industrial advances in the rapidly evolving field of 3D printing and additive manufacturing. The journal provides comprehensive coverage of novel and improved additive manufacturing processes, materials, modeling and simulation techniques, hybrid manufacturing approaches, and advanced design tools, along with applications in medicine and bioprinting, education, food, architecture, and consumer and industrial products. It also addresses broader issues such as policy and law, intellectual property, safety, environmental impact, economic and social implications, business models, and material performance standards, serving researchers, engineers, policymakers, and industry professionals engaged in the development and responsible adoption of additive manufacturing technologies.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts submitted to 3D Printing and Additive Manufacturing must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere, with approval from all authors. The journal accepts research articles, reviews, and case studies related to additive manufacturing technologies and applications. Submissions are made online through the journals submission system, following the prescribed formatting and ethical guidelines. Authors choosing open access must pay an Article Processing Charge (APC) after acceptance, while subscription-based publication requires no fee. All manuscripts undergo peer review to ensure quality, originality, and relevance.
SUBJECT AREA: Engineering - Industrial And Manufacturing Engineering; Mechanical Engineering; Materials Science - Materials Science (Miscellaneous)

AIM & SCOPE: 4OR publishes high-quality, innovative scientific papers on the theory and applications of Operations Research, serving the international OR community. The journal focuses on rigorous and original research with clear methodological or theoretical contributions and rapid dissemination, while avoiding incremental or metaphor-based variants of existing methods. It features invited state-of-the-art surveys, research papers, industry case studies, education papers, and selected PhD thesis abstracts, emphasizing strong scientific foundations, meaningful applications, and relevance to both academic and industrial operations research.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: or 4OR, authors should prepare and submit original manuscripts that have not been published before or under review elsewhere. All co-authors must approve the submission, and the work must be authorized by the relevant institution. Manuscripts are submitted online via the Editorial Manager system, following the journals specific submission guidelines and formatting requirements (including structure, ethical declarations, and figures/tables). Authors must provide complete editable source files (e.g., Word or LaTeX) and include required information such as author details, abstract, keywords, and any required declarations (authorship, competing interests, funding, etc.). The journal adheres to Springer and COPE ethical standards, requiring honesty and integrity in data, proper citation, and transparency about contributions
SUBJECT AREA: Decision Sciences - Management Science And Operations Research; Mathematics - Applied Mathematics

AIM & SCOPE: The AAPG Bulletin aims to publish high-quality, peer-reviewed research that advances the understanding of petroleum geology and energy-related geosciences. The journal covers topics such as sedimentology, stratigraphy, structural geology, geochemistry, basin analysis, petroleum systems, reservoir characterization, and exploration and production technologies, serving researchers and professionals in academia and the energy industry by promoting the scientific and practical application of geoscience knowledge.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: The AAPG Bulletin accepts original, unpublished research articles in the field of petroleum geology and related geosciences. Manuscripts must not be under consideration elsewhere and should be approved by all authors. Submissions are made online and are subject to a rigorous peer-review process. Authors must follow the journals formatting, ethical, and citation guidelines, and ensure clarity, scientific rigor, and relevance to the energy and geoscience community.
SUBJECT AREA: Earth And Planetary Sciences - Geology; Stratigraphy; Economic Geology

AIM & SCOPE: Focuses on pharmaceutical sciences and technology, including formulation, drug development, analytical methods, and quality assurance.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts submitted online via Springer Editorial Manager; must follow submission formatting rules and ethical standards.
SUBJECT AREA: Pharmacology, Toxicology And Pharmaceutics - Pharmaceutical Science; Medicine - Pharmacology (Medical)

AIM & SCOPE: Publishes research on the science and technology of pharmaceuticals, including drug delivery, pharmacokinetics, pharmaceutics, and regulatory science.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts submitted online via Springer Editorial Manager; must be original, not under review elsewhere.
SUBJECT AREA: Pharmacology, Toxicology And Pharmaceutics - Drug Discovery; Pharmaceutical Science; Biochemistry, Genetics And Molecular Biology - Molecular Medicine

AIM & SCOPE: Publishes original research in textiles, including fiber science, dyeing, finishing, and material testing.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Online submission or as per AATCC instructions; must be original research
SUBJECT AREA: Materials Science - Polymers And Plastics; Chemical Engineering - Process Chemistry And Technology

AIM & SCOPE: Provides reviews and updates on textile science, technology, and industry trends; not focused on original research.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Follows editorial guidelines; typically invited articles or reviews.
SUBJECT AREA: Materials Science - Polymers And Plastics; Materials Science (Miscellaneous)

JOURNAL NAME: Abacus-A Journal Of Accounting Finance And Business Studies
AIM & SCOPE: Publishes research in accounting, finance, and business studies, with theoretical and applied papers.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts submitted via Wiley online submission system; must follow formatting and ethical guidelines.
EDITOR: Stewart Jones, University of Sydney Baljit K. Sidhu, University of Sydney
SUBJECT AREA: Business, Management And Accounting - Accounting; Finance

AIM & SCOPE: Abdominal Radiology publishes clinically relevant original research, reviews, and practiceoriented articles in the field of abdominal medical imaging, including gastrointestinal and genitourinary tract imaging and interventional radiologic procedures, serving the international abdominal radiology community and affiliated societies like the Society of Abdominal Radiology (SAR), European Society of Urogenital Radiology (ESUR), European Society of Gastrointestinal Radiology (ESGAR), and Asian Society of Abdominal Radiology (ASAR).
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere Follow Springer submission guidelines: structured sections, clear ethics statements, figures/tables in required format Submitted via Springer Editorial Manager
SUBJECT AREA: Medicine - Radiology, Nuclear Medicine And Imaging

JOURNAL NAME: Abhandlungen Aus Dem Mathematischen Seminar Der Universitat Hamburg
AIM & SCOPE: This is a peerreviewed pure mathematics journal that publishes original research contributions across central areas of mathematics including algebra, complex analysis and geometry, differential geometry, global analysis, graph theory, discrete mathematics, Lie theory, number theory, and topology. It provides a scholarly forum for highquality mathematical research from the community.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Authors must submit original research that has not been published or submitted elsewhere, following Springers online submission and peerreview process. Manuscripts typically use LaTeX for mathematics and must adhere to Springer formatting and ethical standards.

AIM & SCOPE: AcademiaRevista Latinoamericana de Administracin publishes solid theoretical and empirical research on management, including practical regional cases and literature reviews in areas such as finance, accounting, governance, business and society, public management, strategy and marketing, comparative management and culture, organizational theory and behavior, family firms and entrepreneurship, operations and technology. Articles in Spanish or Portuguese are also translated and published in English.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original and unpublished elsewhere. Submitted via Emeralds online system with required formatting and ethical declarations. Articles should be relevant to management practice and theory. Authors choose open access status at acceptance.
SUBJECT AREA: Business, Management And Accounting - Business And International Management; Strategy And Management

AIM & SCOPE: Academic Emergency Medicine publishes peerreviewed original research, clinical studies, education information, and investigations in emergency medicine, including basic science, clinical practice, policy, diagnostics, therapeutics, and health services research relevant to emergency care
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, not previously published or under review elsewhere. Follow detailed Wiley author guidelines and SAEM submission instructions (including structured abstract, article types, ethics).
SUBJECT AREA: Medicine - Emergency Medicine; Critical Care And Intensive Care Medicine

AIM & SCOPE: Academic Pediatrics is the official peerreviewed journal of the Academic Pediatric Association that strengthens the research and educational foundations of academic general pediatrics, including pediatric education, child health services, health policy, injury and behavioral pediatrics, emergency medicine, environment, and other core childhealth topics.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, not published elsewhere, and not under review at another journal. Submitted via Elseviers online submission system with detailed formatting, ethical and author guidelines available on the journals site.
SUBJECT AREA: Medicine - Pediatrics, Perinatology And Child Health

AIM & SCOPE: Academic Psychiatry is the international peerreviewed journal of the American Association of Chairs of Departments of Psychiatry, the American Association of Directors of Psychiatric Residency Training, the Association for Academic Psychiatry, and the Association of Directors of Medical Student Education in Psychiatry. It publishes original, scholarly work in psychiatry and the behavioral sciences that advances knowledge and stimulates evidencebased progress in areas including psychiatric education and training, leadership and administration, career and professional development, ethics and professionalism, and health and wellbeing within academic psychiatry.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Hybrid publication model articles can be published under subscription or open access after payment of APC. SpringerLink Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. SpringerLink Doubleanonymous peer review is used (authors and reviewers are anonymized). SpringerLink Accepted article types, formatting rules, ethical disclosures, and other detailed author instructions are provided on the Springer submission guidelines page.

AIM & SCOPE: Academic Radiology publishes original reports of clinical and laboratory investigations in diagnostic imaging and related technologies, including the use of radioactive isotopes, CT, PET, MRI, ultrasound, digital subtraction angiography, imageguided interventions, and associated techniques. It also includes brief technical reports on new methods, stateoftheart reviews, clinical updates, and radiologic education research.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Follow Elsevier submission standards, including structured abstracts, ethical approval statements, and conflicts of interest disclosures. Submitted through Elseviers online submission system with detailed author instructions on the journals homepage
SUBJECT AREA: Medicine - Radiology, Nuclear Medicine And Imaging

AIM & SCOPE: Academy of Management Annals publishes uptodate, indepth, integrative reviews of research advances in management and organizational studies, moving beyond simple literature summaries to critically synthesize, interpret, and challenge existing knowledge while suggesting future research directions in areas such as business, organizational behavior, human resource management, and related fields.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts begin with a brief proposal (not a full article). Proposals are reviewed after submission deadlines and, if accepted, authors are invited to submit full manuscripts. Submissions are made via the Manuscript Central system. No submission fees are charged; rigorous peer review follows
SUBJECT AREA: Business, Management And Accounting - General Business, Management And Accounting; Strategy And Management

AIM & SCOPE: The Academy of Management Journal publishes highquality empirical research that tests, extends, or builds management theory and contributes to management practice across organizational behavior, strategy, human resource management, entrepreneurship, and related disciplines. The research must show strong theoretical contribution and empirical rigor relevant for the management community.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished elsewhere, and not under review. Empirical research must demonstrate strong theoretical value and relevance. Submission via ScholarOne Manuscripts with APAstyle formatting and ethical disclosures
SUBJECT AREA: Business, Management And Accounting - Organizational Behavior And Human Resource Management; Strategy And Management; Business And International Management

AIM & SCOPE: Academy of Management Learning & Education aims to advance understanding of management learning and education by publishing rigorous theoretical, empirical, and review research that addresses how people learn, teach, and administer education in business and organizational settings, including universities, companies, and public/nonprofit organizations. The journal covers topics such as management teaching, curricula, program evaluation, and issues affecting the institutional environment of business schools and stakeholder
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Academy of Management Learning & Education (AMLE) publishes scholarly work on management learning and education, including empirical research, conceptual papers, reviews, essays, dialogues, and book/resource reviews that contribute to theoretical debate and practice. Submissions must be original and not under review elsewhere, and should follow AOMs style and ethical standards.
SUBJECT AREA: Business, Management And Accounting - Organizational Behavior And Human Resource Management; Strategy And Management

AIM & SCOPE: Academy of Management Review publishes theoretical insights that advance understanding of management and organizations, emphasizing the development, extension, or critical evaluation of formal theory. Submissions aim to create novel conceptual contributions, challenge existing assumptions, or synthesize ideas into new theoretical frameworks in business and organizational studies
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts submitted to Academy of Management Review must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere, and they must extend theory in ways that develop testable, novel, or significantly advanced conceptual contributions. The journal publishes theoretical articles, review essays, and dialogues that contribute to management and organization theory. Manuscripts are submitted online via the ScholarOne Manuscripts system, prepared according to the AOM Style Guide for Authors with all identifying information removed for doubleblind review. Work must not be posted publicly during review, and authors should be willing to participate in peer review when asked. Review Essays and Dialogues have specific length and content requirements (e.g., about 25-30 doublespaced pages for full articles).
SUBJECT AREA: Business, Management And Accounting - Strategy And Management

AIM & SCOPE: Acarologia publishes original research and reviews on all aspects of acarology (the study of mites and ticks), including taxonomy, molecular biology, ecology, evolution, physiology, behaviour, biogeography, genetics, morphology, and applied studies (e.g., plant parasites, biological control, veterinary aspects). It also accepts short notes and long reviews relevant to the discipline.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Original research, reviews, and short notes in acarology are accepted. Manuscripts should comply with formatting (typed, doublespaced, English). Taxonomic papers should ideally include multiple species descriptions unless scientifically justified. Manuscripts not meeting format requirements are rejected without review. Editors may accept PCI recommendations without further review if criteria are met
SUBJECT AREA: Agricultural And Biological Sciences - Insect Science

AIM & SCOPE: Accident Analysis & Prevention publishes research on accidental injury and damage, covering the preinjury and immediate postinjury phases, and includes medical, legal, economic, educational, behavioral, theoretical, and empirical aspects of transportation and other accidents.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Authors should follow Elseviers submission guidelines (structured abstract, ethics statements, conflicts of interest). Submission through Elseviers Editorial System with required formatting and referencing. (Elsevier author instructions apply)
SUBJECT AREA: Medicine - Public Health, Environmental And Occupational Health; Transportation

AIM & SCOPE: Accounting Horizons publishes rigorous academic research and thoughtprovoking perspectives that bridge theory and practice in accounting. It covers broad topics such as financial reporting, corporate governance, assurance services, accounting information systems, data analytics, enterprise risk management, diversity and inclusion in accounting, sustainability, taxation, and regulatory developments. The journal welcomes original research as well as commentaries and perspectives that are relevant to both academics and practitioners
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Accounts should be clear and accessible, with relevance to accounting practice and research. Submitted online through the AAA submission system with standard academic formatting and ethical declarations. Rapid review process with expedited consideration for especially timely topics.

AIM & SCOPE: AOS is an international interdisciplinary journal focusing on the *relationships among accounting, human behavior, organizational and institutional processes, and the sociopolitical environment of enterprises, publishing highquality conceptual and empirical research in accounting, organizational studies, information systems, applied psychology, and related fields
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere (follow Elsevier/ScienceDirect author guidelines). Submission via Elseviers online system (Editorial Manager/Elsevier) with structured formatting, abstracts, keywords, references, ethical statements, etc
SUBJECT AREA: Business, Management And Accounting - Accounting; Organizational Behavior And Human Resource Management

AIM & SCOPE: The Accounting Review publishes rigorous scholarly research in all areas of accounting, including financial accounting, management accounting, auditing, taxation, accounting information systems, and related methodologies, with emphasis on advancing knowledge and informing practice and regulation across disciplines
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: The Accounting Review requires authors to prepare and submit original research manuscripts that have not been submitted elsewhere and meet high standards of rigor, significance, and contribution to accounting research. Manuscripts are submitted through the Editorial Manager system with a separate title page to ensure anonymity, and authors must provide valid ORCID IDs and complete a conflict of interest (COI) disclosure form upon submission. A nonrefundable submission fee is required ($270 for AAA members, $645 for nonmembers). The journal uses a doubleblind peer review process where the senior editor first assesses format and fit, then sends appropriate manuscripts to at least two independent reviewers, with revisions and editorial decisions continuing until final acceptance. Authors must also disclose any potential conflicts of interest, follow ethical guidelines, and adhere to requirements related to survey/experimental reporting and human subjects protections.

AIM & SCOPE: Accounts of Chemical Research publishes concise, authoritative accounts of fundamental research developments in chemistry, biochemistry, materials science, nanoscience, and related interdisciplinary fields. It emphasizes overview articles (Conspectus) that summarize current research trends and provide insight for specialists and nonspecialists alike. Most articles are solicited by invitation, though unsolicited proposals are considered
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts (often Conspectus articles) are usually solicited by the Editor, though unsolicited proposals may be submitted for consideration. Manuscript preparation guidelines (format, structure, lengths) and ethical policies are available in the ACS Author Guidelines (ACS Researcher Resources
SUBJECT AREA: Chemistry - Analytical Chemistry; Physical And Theoretical Chemistry

AIM & SCOPE: The journal focuses on quality assurance, accreditation, and metrology across diverse disciplines including analytical chemistry, safety, reliability and quality systems, instrumentation, chemical engineering, and related measurement science topics. It serves researchers, practitioners, and decisionmakers concerned with quality, traceability, uncertainty, and compliance in laboratory settings and standards implementation.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submitted via Springers online editorial system. Follow Springer formatting, ethical requirements, and peerreview standards.

AIM & SCOPE: ACI Materials Journal publishes peerreviewed research on materials in concrete and construction, including material properties, durability, performance of concrete and related materials, and testing/characterization relevant to civil engineering and structural applications
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and within ACIs scope. Submit via Manuscript Central / ScholarOne. English language, correct spelling, grammar, and formatting. Based on ACI Publication Policy.
SUBJECT AREA: Engineering - Civil And Structural Engineering; Building And Construction

AIM & SCOPE: ACI Structural Journal publishes peerreviewed research and technical papers related to structural concrete design, analysis of concrete elements and systems, and theory and practice of reinforced and prestressed concrete structures. The journal features structural research that advances knowledge for design and practice in structural and civil engineering
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original and unpublished elsewhere. Submit via Manuscript Central (ScholarOne) (ACI structural engineering editorial system). ACI Foundation Follow ACIs instructions for formatting, figures/tables, citations, and technical review. Peer review is conducted by structural and concrete research experts
SUBJECT AREA: Engineering - Civil And Structural Engineering; Building And Construction

AIM & SCOPE: ACM Computing Surveys publishes comprehensive survey and tutorial articles that summarize and analyze broad areas of computer science and computing research highlighting developments, trends, and challenges across theory, methods, systems, and applications. It is widely regarded as a top survey journal in computing
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts (surveys/tutorials) must be original, comprehensive, and thoughtfully scoped for a broad audience in computing. Submitted through ACMs manuscript submission system with full citation, structured survey organization, and often with editorial preapproval. ACM has strict peer review and editorial standards typical of top journals.
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - General Computer Science; Computer Science (Miscellaneous)

AIM & SCOPE: ACM SIGCOMM Computer Communication Review (CCR) is the online magazine and peerreviewed publication of ACM SIGCOMM that publishes technical papers, educational papers, and editorial notes on computer communications and networking. It serves as a forum for novel ideas and emerging research within SIGCOMMs field of interest, emphasizing timely innovation, practical advances, feasibility studies, and readability to foster further investigations in networking systems and communications
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Submission types: Short technical papers (strict page limit) Repeatable technical papers (with artefacts: data/software) Education papers Editorial notes CCR Online Review criteria: Innovation, timeliness, technical integrity and readability. CCR Online Strict submission guidelines (e.g., 6 pages for short papers, artefact criteria for repeatable papers).
CO-EDITOR: Gianni Antichi (Queen Mary University of London, UK) Olivier Bonaventure (Universit catholique de Louvain, Belgium) Joseph Camp (SMU, USA) Matthew Caesar (UIUC, USA)
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Computer Networks And Communications

AIM & SCOPE: The journal publishes original, highquality research on algorithms that are inherently discrete and finite, emphasizing rigorous analysis, mathematical content, and innovations in algorithm design, data structures, complexity results, and related theory and applications (e.g., combinatorial searches, optimization, randomization, parallel and distributed computation)
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submit quarterly via the ACM Digital Library submission system following ACMs author templates (singlecolumn format for review, then ACM style after acceptance). Authors must follow ACMs submission templates (Word/LaTeX) and ethical guidelines.
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Computational Theory And Mathematics; Software

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Architecture And Code Optimization
AIM & SCOPE: Research in computer architecture and code optimization hardware/software interaction, compilers, systems
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Original work via ACM Digital Library system.

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Asian And Low-Resource Language Information Processing
AIM & SCOPE: Language processing for Asian and lowresource languages (NLP, computational linguistics).
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Original research with standard ACM formatting.
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Artificial Intelligence; Language And Linguistics

AIM & SCOPE: Computational and logic aspects of computer science.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Submitted manuscripts should not be published or simultaneously submitted to another journal or to a conference. Full (extended) versions of important, published, conference papers are welcome. Upon acceptance of an article by the journal, the author(s) will be asked to transfer copyright of the article to the ACM. Submissions longer than 45 pages in the ACM style file format are discouraged due to the strain on the TOCL reviewer pool. To ensure timely processing of the submissions, whenever a paper needs to be revised, the authors will be asked to prepare the revised version within six months of receiving the reviews. Many latex systems generate pdf files that are not properly handled by our document tracking system (ManuscriptCentral). Prior to submitting your pdf files, please follow the instructions for distilling pdf files into forms appropriate for ManuscriptCentral.
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Computational Theory And Mathematics

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Embedded Computing Systems
AIM & SCOPE: The design of embedded computing systems, both the software and hardware, increasingly relies on sophisticated algorithms, analytical models, and methodologies. ACM Transactions on Embedded Computing Systems (TECS) aims to present the leading work relating to the analysis, design, behavior, and experience with embedded computing systems
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submitted via ACMs manuscript submission system with ACM style formatting. Standard peer review (rigorous, multireviewer) ensures high quality. Detailed instructions available on the ACM TECS homepage (ACM Digital Library). (ACM submission portal gives specific format requirements.)
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Hardware And Architecture; Embedded Systems

AIM & SCOPE: ACM Transactions on Graphics (TOG) is the foremost peer-reviewed journal in the graphics field. In the colorful pages of TOG, leading researchers discuss breakthroughs in computer-aided design, synthetic image generation, rendering, solid modeling, and other areas. "Research," the largest regular section, is necessary intellectual nourishment for anyone implementing graphics systems
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submissions are handled via ACMs Manuscript Central / ACM submission portal. Papers are reviewed by multiple experts (typically doubleblind review) and should follow ACM author templates (LaTeX/Word). High emphasis on technical novelty, rigor, clarity, and reproducibility in graphics research.
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Computer Graphics And Computer-Aided Design

AIM & SCOPE: TOIS publishes original highquality research in information systems and information retrieval, focusing on the design, evaluation, and understanding of systems that help users find, organize, analyze, and use information across media. Topics include search engines, data management, recommender systems, retrieval models, and related computational techniques
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submitted via ACMs manuscript submission system with ACM style formatting. Standard peer review (rigorous, multireviewer) ensures high quality. Detailed instructions available on the ACM TECS homepage (ACM Digital Library). (ACM submission portal gives specific format requirements.)

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Intelligent Systems And Technology
AIM & SCOPE: ACM TIST publishes original, highquality research on intelligent systems and related technologies, covering areas in artificial intelligence, intelligent agents, humancentered AI, machine learning, and information systems applications from a multidisciplinary perspective.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submit via the ACM manuscript submission system following ACMs author formatting (LaTeX/Word) and peer review policies. Doubleblind or multireviewer rigorous review is standard (specific timelines vary).

AIM & SCOPE: ACM TOIT publishes original research on foundational and applied Internet technologies, spanning Internet architecture, protocols, web technologies, distributed/cloud systems, IoT, security/privacy, performance, data analytics, networking, and related areas that advance design, analysis, and implementation of Internet systems and services
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts should be original, unpublished and not under review elsewhere. Submission is via ACM Manuscript Central / ACM submission portal. Peer review is rigorous multireviewer evaluation. Average review cycles are typically several months per review round
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Computer Networks And Communications

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Knowledge Discovery From Data
AIM & SCOPE: TKDD publishes original archival research on knowledge discovery from data, data mining, and related disciplines, covering scalable algorithms, data analysis, big data mining, text/web/semistructured data mining, spatial/temporal data mining, social network analysis, and other advanced data discovery topics.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. ACM Digital Library Submit via ACM Manuscript Submission System (Editorial Manager) with ACM formatting (LaTeX/Word). ACM Digital Library Starting June1, 2024, TKDD transitioned to doubleanonymous peer review (reviewers dont know authors identities). ACM Digital Library Peer review is rigorous, with multiple expert reviewers evaluating technical soundness and novelty.
SUBJECT AREA: Computer Science - Artificial Intelligence; Information Systems

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Modeling And Computer Simulation
AIM & SCOPE: TOMACS publishes original research and developmental results in computer simulation and modeling, including discreteevent simulation, continuous and combined simulation, Monte Carlo methods, and simulation methodology and tools. It serves as the archival journal for simulation research across computing and applied sciences.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submission via ACMs manuscript system (ACM Digital Library). Peer review typically involves multiple expert reviewers assessing technical quality, novelty, and contribution to simulation research. Formatting conforms to ACM author templates (Word/LaTeX).

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Multimedia Computing Communications And Applications
AIM & SCOPE: TOMM is the flagship archival journal of the ACM Special Interest Group on Multimedia (SIGMM). It publishes highquality research on multimedia computing, communications, and applications, including areas such as multimedia systems, streaming, coding, processing, networking, databases, and realtime multimedia technologies. Papers whose primary focus is only current commercial products are usually not included; emphasis is on scientific and engineering novelty and lasting research contributions.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Submitted via ACMs manuscript submission system (ACM Digital Library). Peer review is rigorous and multistage, with multiple expert reviewers. Author templates and ethical policies follow ACM publication standards (LaTeX/Word).

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Privacy And Security
AIM & SCOPE: TOPS publishes highquality original research on privacy and security in computing and information systems. It covers security technologies (authentication, authorization, intrusion detection, cryptographic protocols), privacypreserving methods, secure systems and networks, risk analysis, assurance, models of trust, and related theoretical and applied topics
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. ACM Digital Library Use ACM author templates (Word/LaTeX) and submit via the ACM Digital Library submission portal. ACM Digital Library Rigorous peer review by multiple experts ensures scientific correctness and relevance. S-Logix Tutorials or survey articles are generally not considered focus is on research with practical or theoretical contributions

JOURNAL NAME: Acm Transactions On Reconfigurable Technology And Systems
AIM & SCOPE: ACM Transactions on Reconfigurable Technology and Systems (TRETS) is a peer-reviewed and archival journal covering technology, systems, and applications on reconfigurable computers or devices. The journal has been the vehicle for many seminal papers over the past decade.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be original, unpublished, and not submitted elsewhere. Submit via ACM Digital Library Manuscript Central. Reviewed by anonymous experts (typically 3 reviewers). Papers with significant extensions beyond prior conference versions (>30% new material) are accepted for review
CO-EDITOR: Philip Leong, Jeff Arnold, Jason D. Bakos
SUBJECT AREA: Engineering - Electrical And Electronic Engineering; Hardware And Architecture

AIM & SCOPE: The ACM Transactions on Sensor Networks (TOSN) serves as a central, archival venue for the interdisciplinary sensor network research community. It covers research contributions that introduce new concepts, techniques, analyses, or architectures, as well as applied contributions that report on development of new tools and systems or experiences and experiments with high-impact, innovative applications. The Transactions places special attention to contributions on systemic approaches to sensor networks.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Submission guidelines: TOSN accepts original research articles, survey/tutorial papers, and technical notes that offer novel contributions. ACM Digital Library Work must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. Manuscripts are submitted via the ACM Digital Library submission system in ACM format (LaTeX/Word). GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Articles are review articles synthesizing broad literature, not primary research papers. AIM & SCOPE: JACC: Heart Failure is a specialty journal of the Journal of the American College of Cardiology (JACC) that publishes high-impact research on the diagnosis, pathophysiology, treatment, and management of heart failure, including clinical outcomes, trials, and advanced therapies.

Original research 5,000 words, reviews 7,500 words, research letters 1,000 words, and letters to the editor 400 words. Manuscripts require a cover letter with ICMJE statements, title page with affiliations and disclosures, ethics approval, and, for some articles, Central Illustration and Clinical Perspectives. The journal uses single-blind peer review and enforces plagiarism checks and compliance with ICMJE and ethical standards.

AIM & SCOPE: The journal aims to publish high-quality research in economics and statistics, combining theoretical, empirical, and applied studies. It focuses on contributions that advance understanding of economic theory, policy, econometrics, and statistical methods. JBNST welcomes papers that develop new methodologies, analyze empirical data rigorously, or provide insights into national and international economic issues. The journal targets researchers, policymakers, and academics interested in quantitative and analytical approaches to economics, with emphasis on clarity, rigor, and originality.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: The journal Jahrbcher fr Nationalkonomie und Statistik (JBNST) publishes original research in economics and statistics, including theoretical, empirical, and applied studies. Manuscripts should include a title page with authors, affiliations, and ORCID IDs, an abstract summarizing objectives, methods, and results, keywords and classification codes, and the main text in English or German with clearly formatted tables, figures, references, equations, and symbols. Only original work is accepted, and plagiarism or duplicate submission is not allowed. Optional supplementary material may be included. After acceptance, authors receive proofs for final corrections. Hybrid open access is available, with potential article processing charges. Authors should follow the journal style consistently, and recent issues can be used as formatting references.
EDITOR: EditorinChief Prof. Dr. Peter Winker, Universitt Gieen.
CO-EDITOR: Prof. Dr. ThiessBttner, Universitt ErlangenNrnberg, Prof. ReginaT.Riphahn,Ph.D., Universitt ErlangenNrnberg, Prof. Dr. WernerSmolny, Universitt Ulm, Prof. Dr. JoachimWagner, Universitt Lneburg.

JOURNAL NAME: Jaids-Journal Of Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndromes
AIM & SCOPE: JAIDS publishes peer-reviewed research on HIV/AIDS, including basic science, clinical studies, treatment, prevention, epidemiology, and public health, to advance understanding and management of HIV infection.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: JAIDS accepts manuscripts in English across original research, clinical studies, reviews, and short reports related to HIV/AIDS. Manuscripts must follow the journals formatting and style requirements, including structured abstracts, references, tables, and figures. All submissions undergo rigorous peer review. Accepted articles may be published online ahead of print. Authors have the option to publish via open access under the hybrid model (APC applies), or under subscription with no fee. Ethical standards, conflict of interest disclosure, and adherence to reporting guidelines (e.g., CONSORT, STROBE) are required. Submission is through the journals online system.

AIM & SCOPE: JAIDS publishes peer-reviewed research on HIV/AIDS, including basic science, clinical studies, treatment, prevention, epidemiology, and public health, to advance understanding, management, and control of HIV infection worldwide.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be submitted in English through the journals online submission system. The journal accepts original research, clinical studies, reviews, and short reports related to HIV/AIDS. Manuscripts should follow the journals formatting and style requirements, including structured abstracts, references, tables, and figures. All submissions undergo rigorous peer review, and accepted articles may be published online ahead of print. Authors may choose open access under the hybrid model by paying an APC after acceptance, while subscription publication requires no fee. Ethical standards, conflict of interest disclosure, and adherence to reporting guidelines such as CONSORT or STROBE are mandatory.

AIM & SCOPE: JAMA Dermatology publishes high-quality research in clinical, surgical, and investigative dermatology, aiming to advance understanding, diagnosis, treatment, and patient care in skin diseases. The journal covers clinical studies, trials, epidemiology, health policy, and innovations in dermatologic care, prioritizing research with clear relevance to patient outcomes and public health.
EDITOR: Kanade Shinkai, MD, PhD, San Francisco, California.
CO-EDITOR: Adewole Adamson, MD, MPP Deputy Editor & Web Editor.

AIM & SCOPE: Facial Plastic Surgery & Aesthetic Medicine publishes peer-reviewed research on reconstructive and cosmetic surgery of the head and neck. It promotes innovation, education, and global collaboration among physicians and providers, and provides a forum for ethics and public policy in facial plastic surgery. It is the official journal of the American Academy of Facial Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery, with affiliated international organizations.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Author Guidelines: Manuscripts must be in English, clearly structured, and follow AMA style. All submissions undergo peer review, adhere to ethical standards for human/animal research, include conflict-of-interest disclosures, and are submitted online via the journals submission system.
EDITOR: Travis T. Tollefson, MD, MPH, FACS (EditorinChief)

AIM & SCOPE: AMA Neurology publishes high-quality, peer-reviewed research in all areas of neurology. The journal aims to advance understanding, diagnosis, and treatment of neurological diseases, improve patient care, and inform clinical practice and policy. It covers clinical trials, epidemiology, neuroscience research, neuroimaging, health policy, ethics, and innovations in neurologic care.
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Author Guidelines: Manuscripts must be in English, clearly structured, and follow AMA style. All submissions undergo peer review, adhere to ethical standards for human/animal research, include conflict-of-interest disclosures, and are submitted online via the journals submission system. Reporting guidelines (e.g., CONSORT, PRISMA) should be followed when applicable.

CO-EDITOR: Associate Editors / Section Editors: The journal has a large editorial board covering neurology subfields (e.g., cerebrovascular disease, epilepsy, neuroimmunology, neurodegenerative disorders). They function like coeditors in handling manuscripts for their specialty areas.

AIM & SCOPE: Neurology, applied medicine

GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Authors should submit manuscripts in English online. Articles must adhere to high ethical standards, peerreview criteria, and AMA style. Research articles may be made immediate open access upon payment of APC; otherwise they become free after 12months. Accepted manuscripts can be deposited in repositories.

GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Manuscripts must be in English and submitted online; they undergo peer review. AIM & SCOPE: Journal of Youth and Adolescence provides a single, high-level medium of communication for psychologists, psychiatrists, biologists, criminologists, educators, and researchers in many other allied disciplines who address the subject of youth and adolescence. The journal publishes quantitative analyses, theoretical papers, and comprehensive review articles. The journal especially welcomes empirically rigorous papers that take policy implications seriously. Research need not have been designed to address policy needs, but manuscripts must address implications for the manner society formally (e.g., through laws, policies or regulations) or informally (e.g., through parents, peers, and social institutions) responds to the period of youth and adolescence.

AIM & SCOPE: The Journal of Adult Development is an interdisciplinary journal covering development in early adulthood, midlife, and later adulthood. The Journal supports innovative theoretical and empirical articles that help direct the future of our field. Critical issues include the importance of life-long education, work, intergenerational relations, and physical and mental health influencing adult development. In addition, the impact of personality, emotions, cognition, and biomarkers on developmental changes are areas of interest. The Journal of Adult Development emphasizes the importance of interindividual differences and contextual issues influencing adult development. Manuscripts covering interventions that promote optimal development throughout the adult life span are also welcome.

AIM & SCOPE: The Journal of African Media Studies (JAMS) is an interdisciplinary journal that provides a forum for debate on the historical and contemporary aspects of media and communication in Africa. It hereby aims to contribute to the on-going re-positioning of media and cultural studies outside the Anglo-American axis. JAMS interprets media in a broad sense, incorporating not only formal media such as radio, television, print, internet and mobile telephony but also considers articles on 'informal', 'small' or 'indigenous' media such as music, jokes and theatre. All articles are double-blind peer-reviewed in order to maintain the highest standards of scholastic integrity.

Kafkas University Veterinary Faculty Journal publishes peer-reviewed articles on veterinary medicine and animal science, including research papers, short communications, case reports, and letters. The journal is open access, so all articles are free to read and download. All submissions are checked for plagiarism, and plagiarism or self-plagiarism is not accepted.
Publishes research on veterinary medicine, animal health, and applied medical sciences.
Kaohsiung Journal of Medical Sciences publishes peer-reviewed research covering all areas of clinical and biomedical sciences, including basic research, translational medicine, and clinical studies.

Kardiologia Polska publishes peer-reviewed articles covering cardiology and cardiovascular medicine, including clinical research, diagnostics, treatment, prevention, and guidelines related to heart diseases.

The Kennedy Institute of Ethics Journal publishes peer-reviewed scholarly articles on bioethics, including ethical issues in medicine, health policy, biotechnology, and public health, with interdisciplinary perspectives.

Kew Bulletin is an international peer-reviewed journal for the taxonomy, systematics and conservation of vascular plants and fungi. Papers on conservation, evolution, palynology, cytology, anatomy, biogeography and phytochemistry are considered, where relevant to taxonomy and systematics. Review papers on topics appropriate to the journal are invited. The journal is richly illustrated with line drawings and photographs, and also features a Book Review and Notices section.

Kidney & Blood Pressure Research publishes peer-reviewed articles on nephrology, hypertension, renal physiology, kidney diseases, and cardiovascularrenal interactions, including clinical and experimental research.

Nephrology, renal physiology, structural and biophysics, pathology, immunology, genetics, and clinical nephrology. It publishes research on acute kidney injury, chronic kidney disease, and transplantation.

Kidney International Supplements publishes peer-reviewed supplementary issues, including clinical practice guidelines, consensus statements, and reports related to nephrology, kidney disease, dialysis, transplantation, and global kidney health.

Kindheit und Entwicklung publishes peer-reviewed research on child and adolescent development, including psychological, educational, social, and clinical aspects of childhood and developmental processes.

AIM & SCOPE: Kinematics and Physics of Celestial Bodies publishes peer-reviewed research on celestial mechanics, astrophysics, orbital dynamics, space physics, and related theoretical and observational studies.
Kinesiology publishes peer-reviewed research in kinesiology, sport science, physical education, biomechanics, motor control, exercise physiology, and related interdisciplinary fields.

The Belgian Journal of Zoology publishes high-quality, original research across all aspects of zoology and animal sciences, including anatomy, behaviour, developmental biology, ecology, evolution, genetics/genomics, and physiology. It welcomes hypothesis-driven studies and reviews that address broad biological questions and insights beyond purely descriptive cataloguing. Manuscripts on veterinary, agricultural, or human medical topics fall outside its scope
GUIDE LINES OF JOURNAL: Authors must submit original, hypothesis-driven research or reviews in English. Manuscripts undergo peer review by at least two referees, and the journal requires clarity, novelty, and scientific rigor. As a diamond open access journal, all accepted content is freely available online with authors retaining copyright typically under Creative Commons licensing
